description_long: This is a two-story wooden cabin with a prominent, wide gable roof made of overlapping wooden shingles. The upper floor features a large balcony with wooden railings that wraps around the front and one side, while the lower level is constructed from stone blocks and includes two small windows and a central garage-like door. The front facade is characterized by large glass windows framed with wood, providing ample natural light to the upper floor. The side views reveal a mix of wood and stone construction, with the stone base supporting the wooden upper structure. There are visible exterior doors on both the front and side, and the overall design suggests a rustic, alpine chalet style suitable for mountainous or forested urban neighborhoods.
description: A rustic two-story wooden cabin with a wide gable roof, stone base, large front windows, and a wraparound balcony.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large balcony, and stone base with two small windows and a central door.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stone foundation, wooden upper structure, and the overhanging roof with minimal window visibility.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a full wall of large glass windows on the upper floor and the continuation of the balcony.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a visible side door, balcony, and stone base, showing less window area.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with wide overhangs and wooden shingle cladding
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple large rectangular windows grouped together on the front and rear upper facades, with two small square windows symmetrically placed on the lower stone base at the front; overall arrangement is symmetrical and emphasizes natural light.
attribute_door_placement: One main double door centered on the front facade, and a single side door on the right side of the cabin; a large garage-style door is also present on the lower front.
